audit_items.proof_trace: The function initializes `ss=NULL` and assigns it only within `if(a)` (line 1959), explicitly acknowledging `tc->owner` (allocation) may be NULL. However, at lines 1984-1986 (`if (!skip) { ++(ss->peer_sent_packets); ss->peer_sent_bytes += bytes; }`), `ss` is dereferenced without any NULL guard. The sibling function `tcp_peer_input_handler` guards every `ss` access with `if(ss)` (lines 1930-1933, 1938-1941, 1943-1945); this function omits the guard in the `!skip` block, creating a NULL pointer dereference if `tc->owner` is NULL.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker triggers RFC6062 TCP data delivery when the allocation lifecycle is in a cleanup/teardown edge case (e.g., session timeout concurrent with in-flight TCP data), causing `tc->owner` to be NULL; the callback fires, `ss` remains NULL, and the unconditional `ss->peer_sent_packets` dereference at line 1985 crashes the TURN server process (denial of service).
